name: Nacimiento del río Mundo y Puerto de Bellotar — circular desde Riópar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Riópar are set within the Sierra del Segura mountains, characterized by diverse and rugged terrain. The region features dramatic karstic landscapes, including extensive limestone formations and deep sinkholes. River valleys, such as those carved by the Mundo River, offer varied elevations and picturesque routes for cyclists. This area provides a challenging yet rewarding environment for exploring on two wheels. Best touring cycling routes around Riópar The most popular touring cycling route is Nacimiento del río Mundo y Puerto de Bellotar — circular desde Riópar, a difficult 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route explores the source of the Mundo River and offers significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Batán del Puerto from Riópar and return via the Puerto de las Crucetillas — circular, a difficult 30.6 miles (49.2 km) path. It features varied elevations and scenic views through the Sierra del Segura. Local touring cyclists also love the Quejigal through the Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo, a 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alcaraz, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Riópar is defined by karstic landscapes, the Sierra del Segura mountains, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain passes to more accessible paths. The routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 198 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Riópar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Riópar?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Riópar, offering a diverse range of experiences within the Sierra del Segura mountains. These routes cater to various skill levels, though many feature challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes in Riópar are classified as difficult due to the rugged mountain terrain and significant elevation changes, some sections of the region's cycling network, such as parts of the Vía Verde de la Sierra Alcaraz, offer smoother, more accessible paths. However, the listed touring routes tend to be more challenging. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063919764/quejigal-through-the-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-from-riopar-viejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quejigal through the Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is a 22.8-mile (36.7 km) route with 703 meters of elevation gain, which is still considered difficult but shorter than others.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Riópar?
A: Yes, Riópar offers several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357277438/nacimiento-del-rio-mundo-y-puerto-de-bellotar-circular-desde-riopar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nacimiento del río Mundo y Puerto de Bellotar — circular desde Riópar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 38.7-mile (62.3 km) trail that explores the source of the Mundo River and includes significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by the famous Mundo River waterfall?
A: Yes, the source of the Mundo River, known for its spectacular 86-meter waterfall, is a key attraction in the area. While specific touring routes might not go directly to the base of the waterfall, they often traverse the surrounding area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mundo River crossing – Riópar Viejo Spring loop from Riópar&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 70 km route that explores the river's vicinity.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on a touring cycling route in Riópar?
A: Touring cyclists in Riópar will experience diverse and rugged terrain, primarily within the Sierra del Segura mountains. Expect dramatic karstic landscapes with unique limestone formations, deep sinkholes, and extensive flat-topped mountains. Routes also wind through picturesque river valleys carved by the Mundo and Tus rivers, offering a mix of high forest density and panoramic views from various peaks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike in Riópar?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural and historical points of interest. For instance, the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5588608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riópar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, with its charming setting and castle ruins, can be explored. Many routes pass through or near such historical sites, allowing cyclists to combine natural beauty with cultural discovery.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Riópar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Riópar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging yet rewarding environment, the stunning natural attractions like the Mundo River, and the diverse landscapes of the Sierra del Segura.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced touring cyclists looking for a significant challenge will find several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867102" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucetillas – Mirador La Rodea Grande loop from Puerto del Arenal&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding route spanning over 130 km with substantial elevation gain, offering a true test of endurance and spectacular views.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic views suitable for a cycling stop?
A: Absolutely. The Sierra del Segura is dotted with peaks that provide breathtaking panoramic views. While specific routes might not be named after these peaks, many ascend to high points offering vistas towards Jaén, the Sierra del Agua, and the Calar del Mundo. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357404193/batan-del-puerto-from-riopar-and-return-via-the-puerto-de-las-crucetillas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán del Puerto from Riópar and return via the Puerto de las Crucetillas — circular&lt;/a&gt; features varied elevations and scenic views through the Sierra del Segura, likely including such viewpoints.

Q: What are some notable natural features I might encounter on a touring cycling route in Riópar?
A: Beyond the Mundo River waterfall, you'll encounter the dramatic karstic landscapes of the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima Natural Park, characterized by massive limestone formations, deep sinkholes, and vertical shafts. The region's high forest density, with Mediterranean forests and conifers like black pine, also forms a significant part of the scenic backdrop.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by mountain peaks or summits?
A: Yes, many routes in the Riópar area offer views of or pass near various mountain peaks. While not always directly on the summit, you can often see prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3933686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras&lt;/a&gt;. These provide excellent landmarks and scenic backdrops for your ride.
tips: A great bike ride to the source of Rio Mundo

The Mundo River is one of the most important rivers in the province of Albacete, and its birth is produced from a series of springs that flow from the limestone rocks of the natural park. These springs form a waterfall known as the "Cascada de los Batanes". From its source, the river covers a distance of approximately 130 kilometers to its mouth at the Segura River, in Murcia. Throughout its route, the river forms numerous waterfalls and waterfalls, such as the "Cascada del Hundimiento" and the "Cascada de los Chorros".

The rich landscape of this municipality, and its plant heritage, make it one of the most interesting for nature lovers, who can lose themselves among the streams and creeks that run through its area, such as the Arroyo de Fuente del Tejo, or climb the Picos de La Sagra and El Padrón, from where they can delight in the view of the entire Guadalimar Valley.
Beautiful mountain pass located after the town of Villaverde de Guadalimar. It is demanding and long. Now, it is worth getting to the top and enjoying the views.

The town is located between the Segura and Alcaraz mountain ranges and the Calar del Río Mundo. It is essential to visit the museum of the Royal Factories of San Juan de Alcaraz. Nearby are the Chorros del Río Mundo (source of the Mundo River) with spectacular waterfalls.
